comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Preserve `redCommitSha` during state sync/rebuild.**

Line 103 introduces `redCommitSha`, but `syncStateWithPlan` does not carry this field forward, so `sync`/`repair-state` can silently drop it and lose red-step audit metadata.

 

<details>
<summary>🛠️ Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
diff --git a/tools/delivery/state.ts b/tools/delivery/state.ts
@@
         handoffGeneratedAt:
           previous?.handoffGeneratedAt ?? inferredTicket?.handoffGeneratedAt,
+        redCommitSha:
+          previous?.redCommitSha ?? inferredTicket?.redCommitSha,
         verifiedAt:
           pickVerifiedAt(previous) ??
           pickVerifiedAt(inferredTicket as PersistedTicketFields | undefined),
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
         handoffGeneratedAt:
           previous?.handoffGeneratedAt ?? inferredTicket?.handoffGeneratedAt,
         redCommitSha:
           previous?.redCommitSha ?? inferredTicket?.redCommitSha,
         verifiedAt:
           pickVerifiedAt(previous) ??
           pickVerifiedAt(inferredTicket as PersistedTicketFields | undefined),
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@tools/delivery/types.ts` at line 103, The new optional field redCommitSha on
the delivery state type is not being propagated by syncStateWithPlan, so runs of
sync/repair-state can drop this audit metadata; update the syncStateWithPlan
implementation to carry redCommitSha through whenever present (e.g., when
merging existing state with plan or building the new state object), ensuring any
branches that construct or copy the state include state.redCommitSha (and
preserve its optional-ness), and add/adjust unit tests covering a state with
redCommitSha to prevent regressions.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: summarize by coderabbit.ai -->
<!-- review_stack_entry_start -->

[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/cesarnml/son-of-anton/pull/31)

<!-- review_stack_entry_end -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

## Walkthrough

This PR introduces a new "post-red" workflow stage for test-driven delivery gates. Tickets in `in_progress` status must now run the CI verification step (expected to fail) via the `post-red` command before transitioning to `red_complete`, after which `post-verify` can succeed. The change adds a new `red_complete` ticket status, broadens doc-only classification to include `.json` files alongside `.md`, updates the CLI orchestrator to dispatch the new command, and provides comprehensive test coverage validating gate ordering and state transitions.

<!-- walkthrough_end -->
<!-- tips_start -->

---

**Actionable comments posted: 1** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> tools/delivery/cli-runner.ts (1)
> > `952-973`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_ > > **Reject explicit `skipped` outside the doc-only auto-skip path.** > > This branch only blocks `undefined`, so `post-verify skipped` still records a code ticket as `verified` after `red_complete`. It also slips through when the doc-only policy is `required`, even though the error message says only `clean` or `patched` are valid there. > > >
> Suggested fix > > ```diff > if ( > subagentReviewPolicy === 'required' && > isDocOnly && > outcome === undefined > ) { > throw new Error( > `Ticket ${target.id} requires an explicit post-verify outcome. Pass \`clean\` or \`patched\`.`, > ); > } > + > + if (outcome === 'skipped' && (!isDocOnly || subagentReviewPolicy === 'required')) { > + throw new Error( > + isDocOnly > + ? `Ticket ${target.id} requires an explicit post-verify outcome. Pass \`clean\` or \`patched\`.` > + : `Ticket ${target.id} cannot record \`skipped\` for post-verify on a code ticket. Pass \`clean\` or \`patched\`.`, > + ); > + } > > if (target?.status === 'in_progress' && !isDocOnly) { > throw createWorkflowContractError( > 'workflow.post_verify.requires_post_red', > `Ticket ${target.id} is at status in_progress. Run \`bun run deliver --plan ${state.planPath} post-red ${target.id}\` before post-verify on a code ticket.`, > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In `@tools/delivery/cli-runner.ts` around lines 952 - 973, The branch currently > allows an explicit outcome === 'skipped' to proceed for code tickets; update the > validation so that any explicit 'skipped' is rejected unless it came from the > doc-only auto-skip path (i.e., subagentReviewPolicy === 'skip_doc_only' && > isDocOnly). Specifically, before calling recordPostVerifyImpl, add a guard that > throws a workflow contract error (or the same Error used for invalid outcomes) > when outcome === 'skipped' and not (subagentReviewPolicy === 'skip_doc_only' && > isDocOnly); also ensure the existing policy-required check (subagentReviewPolicy > === 'required' && isDocOnly && outcome === undefined) still enforces only > 'clean' or 'patched' and that you don't let 'skipped' slip through in that case. > Reference functions/values: subagentReviewPolicy, isDocOnly, outcome, > target.status, recordPostVerifyImpl, createWorkflowContractError. > ``` > >
> >
> >
🧹 Nitpick comments (2)
tools/delivery/test/p9-02.test.ts (1)
`122-149`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Fail fast on git setup errors.** `spawnSync()` reports command failures in its return value instead of throwing. Right now a bad `git init`/`commit`/`push` can cascade into a later `isLocalBranchDocOnly(...)` failure that points at the wrong problem.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@tools/delivery/test/p9-02.test.ts` around lines 122 - 149, The test uses spawnSync to run git commands (e.g., git init, git commit, git push) but doesn't check their return values, so failures can cascade and mislead isLocalBranchDocOnly; update the test to check spawnSync's returned object (status, error, stdout/stderr) after each critical git invocation (those using spawnSync in this block) and throw or fail the test immediately when status is non-zero or error is set, including the command, cwd (tempDir/remoteDir) and stderr in the failure message so git setup errors fail fast and point to the real failing command. ```
tools/delivery/cli-runner.ts (1)
`995-1040`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Short-circuit already-red tickets before rerunning verification.** With an explicit ticket id, this wrapper will still run `ci` for a ticket that's already `red_complete`, and then `recordPostRedImpl(...)` drops the result on the floor. That makes reruns expensive and leaves `redCommitSha` stale if the operator made a newer `[red]` commit.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@tools/delivery/cli-runner.ts` around lines 995 - 1040, The code currently re-runs CI and then calls recordPostRedImpl even when the selected ticket is already in status 'red_complete', wasting work and leaving redCommitSha stale; add an early short-circuit after resolving target (using the same ticketId/target variables) that checks if target.status === 'red_complete' and, if so, log a brief message and return state immediately (before calling isDocOnly, runVerify, or recordPostRedImpl) so existing red-complete tickets are no-ops and do not rerun verification. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@tools/delivery/types.ts`: - Line 103: The new optional field redCommitSha on the delivery state type is not being propagated by syncStateWithPlan, so runs of sync/repair-state can drop this audit metadata; update the syncStateWithPlan implementation to carry redCommitSha through whenever present (e.g., when merging existing state with plan or building the new state object), ensuring any branches that construct or copy the state include state.redCommitSha (and preserve its optional-ness), and add/adjust unit tests covering a state with redCommitSha to prevent regressions. --- Outside diff comments: In `@tools/delivery/cli-runner.ts`: - Around line 952-973: The branch currently allows an explicit outcome === 'skipped' to proceed for code tickets; update the validation so that any explicit 'skipped' is rejected unless it came from the doc-only auto-skip path (i.e., subagentReviewPolicy === 'skip_doc_only' && isDocOnly). Specifically, before calling recordPostVerifyImpl, add a guard that throws a workflow contract error (or the same Error used for invalid outcomes) when outcome === 'skipped' and not (subagentReviewPolicy === 'skip_doc_only' && isDocOnly); also ensure the existing policy-required check (subagentReviewPolicy === 'required' && isDocOnly && outcome === undefined) still enforces only 'clean' or 'patched' and that you don't let 'skipped' slip through in that case. Reference functions/values: subagentReviewPolicy, isDocOnly, outcome, target.status, recordPostVerifyImpl, createWorkflowContractError. --- Nitpick comments: In `@tools/delivery/cli-runner.ts`: - Around line 995-1040: The code currently re-runs CI and then calls recordPostRedImpl even when the selected ticket is already in status 'red_complete', wasting work and leaving redCommitSha stale; add an early short-circuit after resolving target (using the same ticketId/target variables) that checks if target.status === 'red_complete' and, if so, log a brief message and return state immediately (before calling isDocOnly, runVerify, or recordPostRedImpl) so existing red-complete tickets are no-ops and do not rerun verification. In `@tools/delivery/test/p9-02.test.ts`: - Around line 122-149: The test uses spawnSync to run git commands (e.g., git init, git commit, git push) but doesn't check their return values, so failures can cascade and mislead isLocalBranchDocOnly; update the test to check spawnSync's returned object (status, error, stdout/stderr) after each critical git invocation (those using spawnSync in this block) and throw or fail the test immediately when status is non-zero or error is set, including the command, cwd (tempDir/remoteDir) and stderr in the failure message so git setup errors fail fast and point to the real failing command. ```
🪄 Autofix (Beta) Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR: - [ ] Push a commit to this branch (recommended) - [ ] Create a new PR with the fixes
